data.title: Whale Liquidates SOL Short Position to Avoid Liquidation
data.description: According to BlockBeats, an on-chain analyst named Yu Jin reported that a whale recently closed a short position of 115,000 SOL as the liquidation price approached. The whale sold the SOL short position at $175 per SOL, totaling $20.13 million. This resulted in a loss of $1.05 million, with a loss rate of 86%. If the position had not been closed in time, it would have faced liquidation.

data.title: The whale who had previously shorted 115,000 SOL closed his position before the liquidation, with a loss of $1.05 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to @EmberCN monitoring, a whale just chose to close the SOL short position before it approached the liquidation price, selling 115,000 SOL short positions at $175, with a total liquidation amount of $20.13 million, a loss of $1.05 million, and a loss rate of 86%. If the position is not closed in time, it will almost be liquidated.

data.title: Eurozone Inflation Expected to Slow Amid U.S. Tariff Concerns
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, European Central Bank Governing Council member Simkus has indicated that inflation in the eurozone is expected to continue slowing, as the region has not yet fully felt the impact of U.S. tariffs. He emphasized the need for further interest rate cuts by the European Central Bank. Despite initial positive economic activity, recent geopolitical trends, including trade threats from U.S. President Donald Trump, are seen as negative developments.

Simkus noted the presence of 'clear anti-inflationary forces' at play and stated that a decision to cut rates in June is evident to him. He also mentioned the possibility of additional rate cuts after June, although the timing remains uncertain. Since June of last year, the European Central Bank has reduced interest rates seven times, with officials expressing readiness to implement further measures as U.S. tariffs pose a threat to economic growth.

data.title: Ethereum Jumps To $2,000 Amid Market Surge – Analyst Says This Resistance Is Next
data.description:
After soaring over 10% on Wednesday, Ethereum (ETH) has jumped past the $2,000 mark for the first time since March, leading some analysts to suggest that the second-largest crypto could reclaim its macro range in the coming days. Related Reading: XRP Is Forms Bullish Reverse Dragon Head Pattern, How High Can Price Go? Ethereum Hits Seven-Week High For the first time in over a month, Ethereum has retested the $2,000 resistance, hitting a seven-week high of $2,075. The King of Altcoin lost this level at the end of March, nosediving to its two-year low of $1,385 in the following weeks. Amid the late-April market recovery, ETH climbed above crucial levels, reclaiming the $1,600-$1,750 zone over the last 14 days
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/ethereum/ethereum-jumps-to-2000-amid-market-surge-analyst-says-this-resistance-is-next/

data.title: Stablecoin protocol Falcon Finance TVL exceeds $260 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Official data shows that the TVL of Falcon Finance, a stablecoin protocol launched by Andrei Grachev, has exceeded 260 million US dollars and is now reported at 260.86 million US dollars. The APY of its staking version token sUSDF is currently reported at 11.45%. Currently, its stablecoin USDF has been launched on Bitfinex, and Falcon Finance has also integrated the WLFI stablecoin USD1.
Earlier news, DWF Labs partner Andrei Grachev issued a statement saying that Falcon Finance will have its points plan and governance tokens.

data.title: Ethereum Leads Cryptocurrency Surge Amid Global Trade Optimism
data.description:
According to PANews, Ethereum has spearheaded a significant rally in the cryptocurrency market this week, driven by easing global trade tensions and optimism surrounding network upgrades. On Friday, Ethereum, the second-largest digital token by market capitalization, surged by 14% to reach $2,490, marking a weekly increase of nearly 33%. This represents the largest weekly gain since 2021, a period characterized by speculative trading and low interest rates that fueled a cryptocurrency boom during the pandemic.

Bitcoin, the largest digital currency, also experienced a rise, climbing 1.7% to $104,336. Valentin Fournier, Chief Research Analyst at BRN, commented on the strong momentum, suggesting it may continue. He noted that new breakthroughs and policy support could potentially attract new buyers to the market.

data.title: Binance adjusts negative margin balance threshold for portfolio
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance will implement a tiered negative balance threshold mechanism for portfolio margin accounts starting at 0:00 (UTC) on May 17, 2025. The higher the VIP level, the higher the negative balance threshold that can be held. If the negative balance of the account exceeds the corresponding threshold, the system will charge interest fees at 0:00 (UTC) every day according to the margin lending daily interest rate. Negative balances are only allowed when the unified maintenance margin rate (uniMMR) is higher than the 105% liquidation line, and interest will affect the uniMMR value. (This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: Gate.io releases new reserve report, with total reserve value reaching US$10.865 billion
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, Gate.io released a new reserve report. As of May 8, the total value of Gate.io reserves reached 10.865 billion US dollars, the total reserve rate reached 128.57%, and the excess reserve value was 2.415 billion US dollars.
In addition, the BTC reserve rate was 137.69%, an increase of 2,802 pieces from the previous period, the ETH reserve rate was 121.36%, an increase of 188,563 pieces from the previous period, and the USDT reserve rate was 102.63%, an increase of 441,334,786 pieces from the previous period.
